name: HonorBox
kicker: For everyone whose buyers live on GitHub
headline: Sell digital products with just Stripe + GitHub.
tagline: 0% platform fee, $0/month, no server. A static storefront on GitHub Pages, checkout through your own Stripe account, and a GitHub Action that delivers every sale while you sleep.
meta_title: Sell digital products with Stripe + GitHub, 0% fee · HonorBox
meta_description: Sell digital products with just Stripe and GitHub Pages. 0% platform fee, $0/month, no server to run. MIT engine; buyers pay you directly on your own Stripe account.
subline: This store runs on the engine it sells. Fork the free core and open your own stand.

items.title: Fork the stand
items.text: Fork the free, MIT-licensed engine and edit one config file. Your storefront builds to GitHub Pages; hosting costs $0, forever.

items.title: Put out your goods
items.text: Create a Stripe Payment Link for each product and drop the URL in the config. Buyers pay you directly on your own Stripe account. Nobody stands between you and your balance.

items.title: Let the box collect
items.text: A scheduled GitHub Action polls Stripe, invites each buyer's GitHub account to your private product repo, and keeps your books. No webhooks, no server, no database.

sections.title: Side by side
sections.note: Fees as published on each vendor's own pricing page, checked July 2026. Polar is the closest comparison: it delivers private-repo access natively and instantly, and it handles your VAT. The row that matters most is the last one. If you want a merchant of record, one of the first three is the better tool, and the guides below say when.

sections.text: Do not buy this if you sell to people without GitHub accounts. Delivery is a repository invite, so that is a hard requirement, not a preference. Do not buy it if you sell a handful of copies a month either: at that volume the fee saving is a couple of dollars, and a merchant of record will handle your VAT for it, which is the better trade. This earns its keep at volume, or when owning the Stripe account and the customer relationship matters to you for its own sake.

items.q: What exactly do I get when I buy?
items.a: Your GitHub account is invited to the product's private repository, usually within minutes and always within a few hours. You keep access permanently, updates included. If the invite doesn't arrive there is a defined recovery path, ending in access or a refund; you are never left holding a receipt for nothing.

items.q: Why is there no instant download?
items.a: Because the product is private-repo access, which a download link can't be: per-buyer grants, clean revocation on refund, updates included forever. A scheduled job sends the invite, usually within minutes, with zero infrastructure to run or break. Sellers who want seconds can turn on the optional webhook mode. A download URL is instant, and then uncontrollable.

items.q: Do my buyers need GitHub accounts?
items.a: Yes. Delivery is a repo invite, so every buyer needs one. Fine for code, templates, courses aimed at technical people, and tools. The wrong channel for lay-reader ebooks, physical goods, or general consumers. Better to know that before your first failed delivery.

items.q: Do I need HonorBox Pro to use the engine?
items.a: No. The free core is a complete store: two themes, checkout, fulfillment, docs. Pro adds reconcile, which pairs every paid order back to whether that buyer actually has access right now, plus the offline license-key module, the premium themes, and multi-product catalog patterns. Buying it funds the free core.

items.q: Does the fulfillment bot need my full Stripe secret key?
items.a: No. A restricted key that can only read Checkout Sessions runs the whole poll; it cannot move money, refund, or create anything. Pair it with a fine-grained GitHub token valid for just the product repo. The least-privilege guide in the docs shows the exact toggles.

items.q: What about VAT and sales tax?
items.a: HonorBox is not a merchant of record. You sell on your own Stripe account, so tax obligations are yours. That's workable for most small sellers under registration thresholds, and Stripe Tax can automate it beyond that. The tax doc walks through what a small seller actually owes.
30 days, no questions asked, refunded through Stripe to your original payment method. Repository access ends when a refund is issued.

HonorBox is an independent project, MIT-licensed, and versioned in the open: every change ships as a tagged release with notes. It is young, and the release history says exactly how young. Support runs through GitHub issues, which are public and tracked, or honorbox@proton.me for anything about your own order.
